releases.title: collectZ v3.8.13
releases.summary: This release makes Capture Inbox easier to work through by adding review filters for captures that need a decision, are ready to add, have no match, are missing details, or have provider/replay problems.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added server-backed `review_filter` support to `GET /api/capture-items`.
Added capture review counts for all review filters.
Added Capture Inbox review tabs: `All`, `Needs choice`, `Ready to add`, `No match`, `Missing details`, and `Problems`.
Kept all capture records on the existing Capture Inbox page instead of creating a separate queue.
Updated OpenAPI to document the new query parameter and response counts.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.12
releases.summary: This release makes Capture Inbox faster for scanning several books or items in a row by adding an explicit batch scan mode.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a `Batch scan` action to Capture Inbox.
Batch mode keeps the editor open and launches the next camera barcode scan after saving.
The editor now shows a compact batch-session readback with saved, added, and review counts.
`Save and scan next` keeps unresolved captures in Capture Inbox for later review.
`Add to library` and `Link` continue to the next scan while batch mode is active.
Normal one-off `New capture` behavior remains available.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.11
releases.summary: This release shortens the Capture Inbox barcode workflow by showing lookup candidates immediately in the new-capture editor after a barcode or ISBN is scanned or entered.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Capture Inbox can run the scanner barcode lookup contract before the capture row is saved.
New-capture lookup results appear inline with candidate title, source, type, and library state.
A selected result can be added or linked through the existing Capture Inbox import path.
Captures saved for review now keep lookup matches and lookup status on the capture row.
Added a `Save and scan next` action so scanning another item does not require returning to the inbox list.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.10
releases.summary: This release makes the Capture Inbox barcode camera flow more useful for books by looking for an ISBN in the captured barcode photo instead of stopping at the retail UPC.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: After the camera barcode action reads a code, Capture Inbox now runs the existing client-side identifier OCR against the same image.
If an ISBN is found, the capture form uses the ISBN, marks the object type as Book, and sets the symbology to `ISBN-13`.
If no ISBN is found, the flow falls back to the detected UPC/barcode as before.
Preserves OCR text readback when available so the capture row can still be reviewed.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.9
releases.summary: This release tightens Capture Inbox on mobile Safari by making the new-capture form scroll correctly inside the dashboard shell and adding a direct camera scan control for barcodes.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Gives Capture Inbox its own scroll container so the iOS Safari form can move past the Title field.
Adds a `Scan` action beside Barcode / ISBN that opens the device camera and decodes the captured image through the existing BarcodeDetector/ZXing client pipeline.
Keeps manual barcode entry and photo upload intact.
Marks photo upload controls as mobile-camera capable.
Adds regression coverage for the mobile scan affordance and scrollable capture editor.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.8
releases.summary: This release lets Capture Inbox turn a selected lookup match into a real library link or import, keeping the user in control of the final decision.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Adds authenticated `POST /api/capture-items/{id}/import-match`.
Reuses the scanner barcode import pipeline so Capture Inbox and the iOS scanner share the same backend source of truth.
Links captures to existing library items when the selected match is already in the catalog.
Imports provider candidates into canonical media rows when the selected match is new.
Marks converted captures with the linked media id and stores selected-match/import readback in capture review metadata.
Adds compact `Link` and `Import` actions beside persisted capture lookup matches.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.7
releases.summary: This release lets Capture Inbox turn an applied barcode or ISBN into reviewable catalog and provider match candidates.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Adds authenticated `POST /api/capture-items/{id}/lookup-matches`.
Reuses the backend scanner barcode lookup path used by `/api/media/lookup/barcode`.
Stores lookup matches and summary counts on the capture row for later review.
Adds a compact `Find matches` action for capture rows that have a barcode or applied OCR candidate.
Shows persisted capture matches inline, including existing catalog matches marked as already in the library.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.6
releases.summary: This release lets Capture Inbox run backend-owned OCR against uploaded photo captures.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Adds authenticated `POST /api/capture-items/{id}/ocr-image`.
Reads locally stored capture uploads from backend storage before calling the configured Vision/OCR provider.
Stores extracted OCR text and reviewable ISBN, UPC/EAN, and ASIN candidates on the capture row.
Adds a compact `Read image text` action for photo captures in Capture Inbox.
Surfaces optional Vision/OCR provider settings through integration config readback and public compose env pass-through.
Records image OCR extraction activity for timeline/readback.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.5
releases.summary: This release turns stored Capture Inbox replay conflicts into a visible review workflow.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Shows unresolved replay conflicts directly on Capture Inbox rows.
Lists the conflicting fields with current and replayed values.
Adds explicit `Keep current` and `Use replayed values` actions.
Adds a scoped backend endpoint to resolve the latest replay conflict without editing raw review metadata.
Records replay conflict resolution activity for timeline/readback.

releases.title: collectZ v3.8.4
releases.summary: This release makes idempotent Capture Inbox retries more reviewable by preserving conflicting replay data instead of silently overwriting the existing capture row.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Detects differing title, capture type, object type, barcode, symbology, OCR text, and image path values when a queued capture retry reuses an existing `client_capture_id`.
Keeps the existing capture values when a replay contains conflicting identifying data.
Stores conflicting incoming values in `capture_items.review_decision.capture_replay_conflicts`.
Returns `idempotency.status`, `idempotency.conflict_count`, and `replay_conflicts` in retry responses.
Documents replay conflict readback in OpenAPI.
